2604.19398.title: GRASPrune: Global Gating for Budgeted Structured Pruning of Large Language Models
2604.19398.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are expensive to serve because model parameters, attention computation, and KV caches impose substantial memory and latency costs. We present GRASPrune, a structured pruning framework applied after pretraining that jointly prunes FFN channels and KV head groups under a single global budget. Instead of learning importance scores without constraints and applying the budget only after training, GRASPrune learns lightweight gate scores with a projected straight-through estimator that enforces a hard mask satisfying the budget at every step while keeping the backbone weights frozen. After the mask is fixed, we calibrate scaling factors on the retained units to mitigate scale mismatch caused by pruning, and fold these factors into the pruned weights to obtain a smaller dense checkpoint with no extra parameters at inference. On LLaMA-2-7B, GRASPrune removes 50% of parameters and achieves 12.18 perplexity on WikiText-2 while maintaining competitive average zero-shot accuracy on five benchmarks, using four epochs on 512 unlabeled calibration sequences on a single NVIDIA A100 80GB GPU without any full model fine-tuning.
2604.19398.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 的服務成本高昂，因為模型參數、注意力計算和 KV 快取會帶來相當大的記憶體和延遲成本。<lb/>我們提出了 GRASPrune，一個結構化剪枝框架，在預訓練後應用，能夠在單一的全局預算下共同剪枝 FFN 通道和 KV 頭組。<lb/>GRASPrune 並不是在沒有約束的情況下學習重要性分數並在訓練後再應用預算，而是使用投影直通估計器學習輕量級的閘門分數，強制執行一個在每一步都滿足預算的硬遮罩，同時保持主幹權重不變。<lb/>在遮罩固定後，我們對保留的單元進行縮放因子的校準，以減輕剪枝造成的縮放不匹配，並將這些因子折疊到剪枝後的權重中，以獲得一個更小的密集檢查點，且在推理時不增加額外的參數。<lb/>在 LLaMA-2-7B 上，GRASPrune 移除了 50% 的參數，並在 WikiText-2 上達到了 12.18 的困惑度，同時在五個基準上保持了具有競爭力的平均零-shot 準確率，使用四個時期在單個 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPU 上對 512 個未標記的校準序列進行訓練，且不需要進行任何完整模型的微調。
2604.19398.author: Ziyang Wang et.al.
2604.19398.authors: Ziyang Wang, Jiangfeng Xiao, Chuan Xiao, Ruoxiang Li, Rui Mao, Jianbin Qin
